content.problemStatement:
Chef is feeling the heat this summer and wants to switch on the Air Conditioning all the time, but since he doesn't want to harm the environment much, he only switches it on when the room temperature is **strictly greater** than 303030 degrees celsius.

Given the room temperature, you need to find whether Chef will switch on the air conditioning or not.


Input Format

The only line of input will contain a single integer TTT - denoting the room temperature.


Output Format
Print `YES` if Chef will turn the Air Conditioning on, otherwise print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YES, yEs, yes, and yeS will all be treated as identical).
constraints: Number of temperatures in the input ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The input consists of one or more integers, each representing the room temperature in degrees Celsius. Integers can be separated by whitespace or newlines.
content.outputFormat: For each temperature, output "YES" if it is strictly greater than 30, otherwise output "NO". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 30 is not greater than 30, so Chef does not turn the AC on.
41 > 30, therefore Chef turns the AC on.

solution.approach: Read every temperature value from the input. For each value, compare it with the threshold 30. If the value is greater, print YES; otherwise print NO. This is a direct O(1) check per temperature.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) where N is the number of temperature values in the input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides input storage.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
out = []
for token in sys.stdin.read().split():
    t = int(token)
    out.append('YES' if t > 30 else 'NO')
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
